html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume One — Part Two

The Torah — First Book

BERESHIT

In the Beginning

Chapters 12 through 25

The Complete Life of Avraham — The Covenant — The Aqedah

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QGena (4Q1) · 4QGenb (4Q2) · 4QGenc (4Q3) · 4QGend (4Q4)

4QGene (4Q5) · 4QGenf (4Q6) · 4QGenh (4Q8) · 4QGenj (4Q10)

1QapGen — Genesis Apocryphon — Cave 1 — primary Aramaic expansion

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE CALL OF AVRAM — CHAPTERS 12 THROUGH 15

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 12 The Call of Avram — The Journey to Kena'an — Egypt

12:1 Now YHWH said to Avram: Go from your country and your kindred and your father's house to the land that I will show you.

12:2 And I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.

12:3 I will bless those who bless you, and him who dishonors you I will curse, and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.

12:4 So Avram went, as YHWH had told him, and Lot went with him. Avram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.

12:5 And Avram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their possessions that they had gathered, and the people that they had acquired in Haran, and they set out to go to the land of Kena'an. When they came to the land of Kena'an,

12:6 Avram passed through the land to the place at Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Kena'ani were in the land.

12:7 Then YHWH appeared to Avram and said: To your offspring I will give this land. So he built there an altar to YHWH, who had appeared to him.

12:8 From there he moved to the hill country on the east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. And there he built an altar to YHWH and called upon the name of YHWH.

12:9 And Avram journeyed on, still going toward the Negev.

12:10 Now there was a famine in the land. So Avram went down to Egypt to sojourn there, for the famine was severe in the land.

12:11 When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife: I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance,

12:12 And when the Egyptians see you, they will say: This is his wife. Then they will kill me, but they will let you live.

12:13 Say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared for your sake.

12:14 When Avram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.

12:15 And when the princes of Pharaoh saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh. And the woman was taken into Pharaoh's house.

12:16 And for her sake he dealt well with Avram — and he had sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male servants, female servants, female donkeys, and camels.

12:17 But YHWH afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Avram's wife.

12:18 So Pharaoh called Avram and said: What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?

12:19 Why did you say: She is my sister, so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife — take her, and go.

12:20 And Pharaoh gave men orders concerning him, and they sent him away with his wife and all that he had.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena (4Q1) and the Genesis Apocryphon (1QapGen) preserve portions of Chapter 12. The 1QapGen significantly expands the Egypt account — it includes Avram's dream warning him about Egypt, a detailed description of Sarai's beauty given by the Egyptians to Pharaoh, and the affliction of Pharaoh's household. The Aramaic text of 1QapGen is the most detailed ancient account of Avram in Egypt.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Yovelim 13:1-13 expands the journey from Haran to Kena'an and notes that Avram built altars at every major stopping point.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:3 — in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed. This is the foundational covenant promise to Avram. It is not given at circumcision — that comes in Chapter 17. It is not conditional on obedience — it is given before any test. The blessing of all families through Avram's seed is the theological anchor of the entire covenant record from this point forward.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:17 — YHWH afflicted Pharaoh with great plagues because of Sarai. The pattern of Egyptian plagues appears first in Avram's story before it appears in Moshe's story. Yovelim 13:13 states explicitly that the plagues on Pharaoh's house in Avram's time foreshadow the plagues of Moshe's time. The two events are theologically connected in the ancient record.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 13 Avram and Lot Separate — The Promise of the Land

13:1 So Avram went up from Egypt, he and his wife and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negev.

13:2 Now Avram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

13:3 And he journeyed on from the Negev as far as Bethel to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,

13:4 To the place where he had made an altar at the first. And there Avram called upon the name of YHWH.

13:5 And Lot, who went with Avram, also had flocks and herds and tents,

13:6 So that the land could not support both of them dwelling together — for their possessions were so great that they could not dwell together,

13:7 And there was strife between the herdsmen of Avram's livestock and the herdsmen of Lot's livestock. At that time the Kena'ani and the Perizzim were dwelling in the land.

13:8 Then Avram said to Lot: Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herdsmen and my herdsmen, for we are kinsmen.

13:9 Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right, or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left.

13:10 And Lot lifted up his eyes and saw that the Jordan Valley was well watered everywhere like the garden of YHWH, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Tzoar. This was before YHWH destroyed Sedom and Amorah.

13:11 So Lot chose for himself all the Jordan Valley, and Lot journeyed east. Thus they separated from each other.

13:12 Avram settled in the land of Kena'an, while Lot settled among the cities of the valley and moved his tent as far as Sedom.

13:13 Now the men of Sedom were wicked, great sinners against YHWH.

13:14 YHWH said to Avram, after Lot had separated from him: Lift up your eyes and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward,

13:15 For all the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever.

13:16 I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted.

13:17 Arise, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you.

13:18 So Avram moved his tent and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron, and there he built an altar to YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 13. The promise of the land at verses 13:14-17 is attested in the DSS fragments.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13:10 — Lot saw the Jordan Valley was well watered like the garden of YHWH, like the land of Egypt. The Jordan Valley is compared to two places: the Garden of Eden and Egypt. Both are understood as places of extraordinary fertility. The comparison to Egypt is significant — Egypt in the ancient record is not presented as inferior or cursed but as a land of abundance. Lot chose well by any worldly measure. What he did not account for was the moral condition of Sedom — verse 13:13.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 14 The War of the Kings — Lot Rescued — Malkhi-Tzedek

14:1 In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Ariokh king of Ellasar, Kedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim,

14:2 These kings made war with Bera king of Sedom, Birsha king of Amorah, Shinav king of Admah, Shemever king of Tzevoyim, and the king of Bela, that is, Tzoar.

14:3 And all these joined forces in the Valley of Siddim, that is, the Salt Sea.

14:4 Twelve years they had served Kedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

14:5 In the fourteenth year Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and defeated the Refaim in Ashterot-Karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-Kiryataim,

14:6 And the Horites in their hill country of Seir as far as El-Paran on the border of the wilderness.

14:7 Then they turned back and came to En-Mishpat, that is, Kadesh, and defeated all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who were dwelling in Hatzatzon-Tamar.

14:8 Then the king of Sedom, the king of Amorah, the king of Admah, the king of Tzevoyim, and the king of Bela, that is, Tzoar, went out, and they joined battle in the Valley of Siddim

14:9 With Kedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Ariokh king of Ellasar — four kings against five.

14:10 Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits, and as the kings of Sedom and Amorah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the hill country.

14:11 So the enemy took all the possessions of Sedom and Amorah, and all their provisions, and went their way.

14:12 They also took Lot, the son of Avram's brother, who was dwelling in Sedom, and his possessions, and went their way.

14:13 Then one who had escaped came and told Avram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshkol and of Aner. These were allies of Avram.

14:14 When Avram heard that his kinsman had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, 318 of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.

14:15 And he divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and defeated them and pursued them to Hovah, north of Damascus.

14:16 Then he brought back all the possessions, and also brought back his kinsman Lot with his possessions, and the women and the people.

14:17 After his return from the defeat of Kedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sedom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh, that is, the King's Valley.

14:18 And Malkhi-Tzedek king of Shalem brought out bread and wine. He was priest of The Creator Most High.

14:19 And he blessed him and said: Blessed be Avram by The Creator Most High, Possessor of heaven and earth.

14:20 And blessed be The Creator Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand. And Avram gave him a tenth of everything.

14:21 And the king of Sedom said to Avram: Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself.

14:22 But Avram said to the king of Sedom: I have lifted my hand to YHWH, The Creator Most High, Possessor of heaven and earth,

14:23 That I would not take a thread or a sandal strap or anything that is yours, lest you should say: I have made Avram rich.

14:24 I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me. Let Aner, Eshkol, and Mamre take their share.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 14. The account of Malkhi-Tzedek at verses 14:18-20 is attested in the DSS.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:13 — Avram the Hebrew. This is the first use of the designation Hebrew — Ivri — in the Torah. It identifies Avram by his lineage from Ever — Bereshit 10:24-25 — and separates him from all the surrounding nations. The word Ivri — one who crosses over — connects to Avram's crossing over from Ur and from the world of idolatry to the covenant with YHWH.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:18 — Malkhi-Tzedek king of Shalem, priest of The Creator Most High. Malkhi-Tzedek is both king and priest — a dual office. He brings bread and wine. He blesses Avram. Avram gives him a tithe of everything. Shalem is the ancient name for Yerushalayim. The Psalm scroll at Qumran — 11QPsa — contains the full Psalm 110 which references Malkhi-Tzedek explicitly. The Qumran document 11QMelch (11Q13) presents Malkhi-Tzedek as a heavenly figure who executes judgment in the last days. This is a figure of extraordinary significance in the ancient Hebrew theology preserved at Qumran.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 15 The Covenant Between the Parts — The Prophecy of Egypt

15:1 After these things the word of YHWH came to Avram in a vision: Fear not, Avram, I am your shield — your reward shall be very great.

15:2 But Avram said: O YHWH The Creator, what will you give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?

15:3 And Avram said: Behold, you have given me no offspring, and a member of my household will be my heir.

15:4 And behold, the word of YHWH came to him: This man shall not be your heir — your very own son shall be your heir.

15:5 And he brought him outside and said: Look toward heaven, and number the stars, if you are able to number them. Then he said to him: So shall your offspring be.

15:6 And he believed YHWH, and he counted it to him as righteousness.

15:7 And he said to him: I am YHWH who brought you out from Ur of the Kasdim to give you this land to possess.

15:8 But he said: O YHWH The Creator, how am I to know that I shall possess it?

15:9 He said to him: Bring me a heifer three years old, a female goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.

15:10 And he brought him all these, cut them in half, and laid each half over against the other. But he did not cut the birds in half.

15:11 And when birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Avram drove them away.

15:12 As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Avram. And behold, dreadful and great darkness fell upon him.

15:13 Then YHWH said to Avram: Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years.

15:14 But I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.

15:15 As for you, you shall go to your fathers in peace — you shall be buried in a good old age.

15:16 And they shall come back here in the fourth generation, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.

15:17 When the sun had gone down and it was dark, behold, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.

15:18 On that day YHWH made a covenant with Avram, saying: To your offspring I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates,

15:19 The land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,

15:20 The Hittites, the Perizzites, the Refaim,

15:21 The Amorites, the Kena'ani, the Girgashites and the Yevusim.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 15 including the covenant between the parts. The prophecy of 400 years in Egypt at verse 15:13 is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Yovelim 14:1-20 expands the covenant ceremony and identifies the specific feast day — Shavuot — on which YHWH made the covenant with Avram.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:6 — he believed YHWH, and he counted it to him as righteousness. This is the foundational statement of covenant faith in the entire Torah. Avram did not yet have a son. He had not yet been circumcised. He had not yet received the full covenant at Chapter 17. He simply believed YHWH's word and YHWH reckoned it as righteousness. The sequence is critical: faith precedes circumcision in Avram's story.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:13 — your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs for four hundred years. This prophecy given to Avram at the covenant between the parts is the theological explanation for everything that happens in Shemot. YHWH told Avram before it happened. The suffering in Egypt was not an accident or a defeat — it was the fulfillment of a covenant prophecy. YHWH's judgment on Egypt in Shemot is the fulfillment of verse 15:14: I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 16 Hagar and Yishma'el — The Angel of YHWH

16:1 Now Sarai, Avram's wife, had borne him no children. She had a female Egyptian servant whose name was Hagar.

16:2 And Sarai said to Avram: Behold now, YHWH has prevented me from bearing children. Go in to my servant — it may be that I shall obtain children by her. And Avram listened to the voice of Sarai.

16:3 So, after Avram had lived ten years in the land of Kena'an, Sarai, Avram's wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her servant, and gave her to Avram her husband as a wife.

16:4 And he went in to Hagar, and she conceived. And when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.

16:5 And Sarai said to Avram: May the wrong done to me be on you. I gave my servant to your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked on me with contempt. May YHWH judge between you and me.

16:6 But Avram said to Sarai: Behold, your servant is in your power — do to her as you please. Then Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she fled from her.

16:7 The angel of YHWH found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.

16:8 And he said: Hagar, servant of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going? She said: I am fleeing from my mistress Sarai.

16:9 The angel of YHWH said to her: Return to your mistress and submit to her.

16:10 The angel of YHWH also said to her: I will surely multiply your offspring so that they cannot be numbered for multitude.

16:11 And the angel of YHWH said to her: Behold, you are pregnant and shall bear a son. You shall call his name Yishma'el, because YHWH has listened to your affliction.

16:12 He shall be a wild donkey of a man, his hand against everyone and everyone's hand against him, and he shall dwell over against all his kinsmen.

16:13 So she called the name of YHWH who spoke to her: You are a Creator of seeing, for she said: Truly here I have seen him who looks after me.

16:14 Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi — it lies between Kadesh and Bered.

16:15 And Hagar bore Avram a son, and Avram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bore, Yishma'el.

16:16 Avram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Yishma'el to Avram.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 16.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:7 — the angel of YHWH found Hagar by a spring in the wilderness. This is the first appearance of the angel of YHWH in the Torah who speaks directly in the first person as YHWH himself — verse 16:10: I will multiply your offspring. The angel of YHWH is the same figure as the Angel of Presence in Yovelim who dictated the book to Moshe. This heavenly messenger acts with full divine authority and speaks with YHWH's own voice throughout the Torah.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:13 — Hagar names YHWH: You are a Creator of seeing. Hagar — an Egyptian woman, a servant — is the first person in the Torah to name YHWH. Not Avram. Not Sarai. An Egyptian servant woman sees YHWH in the wilderness and gives him a name. The ancient record preserves this without apology or qualification.

THE COVENANT OF CIRCUMCISION — CHAPTER 17

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 17 The Covenant of Circumcision — Avram Becomes Avraham

17:1 When Avram was ninety-nine years old YHWH appeared to Avram and said to him: I am The Creator Almighty — walk before me, and be blameless,

17:2 That I may make my covenant between me and you, and may multiply you greatly.

17:3 Then Avram fell on his face. And The Creator said to him:

17:4 Behold, my covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations.

17:5 No longer shall your name be called Avram, but your name shall be Avraham, for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations.

17:6 I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come from you.

17:7 And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your offspring after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be The Creator to you and to your offspring after you.

17:8 And I will give to you and to your offspring after you the land of your sojournings, all the land of Kena'an, for an everlasting possession, and I will be their Creator.

17:9 And The Creator said to Avraham: As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.

17:10 This is my covenant, which you shall keep, between me and you and your offspring after you: Every male among you shall be circumcised.

17:11 You shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.

17:12 He who is eight days old among you shall be circumcised — every male throughout your generations, whether born in your house or bought with your money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring.

17:13 Both he who is born in your house and he who is bought with your money, shall surely be circumcised. So shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant.

17:14 Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people — he has broken my covenant.

17:15 And The Creator said to Avraham: As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.

17:16 I will bless her, and moreover, I will give you a son by her. I will bless her, and she shall become nations — kings of peoples shall come from her.

17:17 Then Avraham fell on his face and laughed and said to himself: Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?

17:18 And Avraham said to The Creator: Oh that Yishma'el might live before you!

17:19 The Creator said: No, but Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Yitzhak. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his offspring after him.

17:20 As for Yishma'el, I have heard you — behold, I have blessed him and will make him fruitful and multiply him greatly. He shall father twelve princes, and I will make him into a great nation.

17:21 But I will establish my covenant with Yitzhak, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this time next year.

17:22 When he had finished talking with him, The Creator went up from Avraham.

17:23 Then Avraham took Yishma'el his son and all those born in his house or bought with his money, every male among the men of Avraham's house, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, as The Creator had said to him.

17:24 Avraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

17:25 And Yishma'el his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

17:26 That very day Avraham and his son Yishma'el were circumcised.

17:27 And all the men of his house, those born in the house and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 17 including the circumcision covenant. The name changes from Avram to Avraham and Sarai to Sarah are attested in the DSS text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Yovelim 15:1-34 expands the circumcision covenant extensively — stating that circumcision is written on the heavenly tablets as an eternal ordinance and that any uncircumcised male belongs to the children of destruction, not the children of the covenant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17:5 — your name shall be Avraham, father of a multitude of nations. The name change is a covenant act. Avraham does not just receive a new name — he receives a new identity. He is no longer the man of Ur, the son of Terach, the husband without an heir. He is the father of nations before a single nation exists. YHWH names what does not yet exist as though it already does.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17:20 — Yishma'el will father twelve princes and become a great nation. YHWH hears Avraham's prayer for Yishma'el and blesses him explicitly. The covenant passes through Yitzhak — but Yishma'el is not abandoned or cursed. He receives his own blessing, his own promise, his own twelve princes. The ancient record is explicit: both sons of Avraham receive divine blessing. The covenant distinction is not a rejection of Yishma'el.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 18 The Three Visitors — Avraham Intercedes for Sedom

18:1 And YHWH appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the door of his tent in the heat of the day.

18:2 He lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing in front of him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the earth

18:3 And said: O YHWH, if I have found favor in your sight, do not pass by your servant.

18:4 Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree,

18:5 While I bring a morsel of bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on — since you have come to your servant. So they said: Do as you have said.

18:6 And Avraham went quickly into the tent to Sarah and said: Quick — three seahs of fine flour. Knead it and make cakes.

18:7 And Avraham ran to the herd and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to a young man, who prepared it quickly.

18:8 Then he took curds and milk and the calf that he had prepared, and set it before them. And he stood by them under the tree while they ate.

18:9 They said to him: Where is Sarah your wife? And he said: She is in the tent.

18:10 YHWH said: I will surely return to you about this time next year, and Sarah your wife shall have a son. And Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.

18:11 Now Avraham and Sarah were old, advanced in years. The way of women had ceased to be with Sarah.

18:12 So Sarah laughed to herself, saying: After I am worn out, and my lord is old, shall I have pleasure?

18:13 YHWH said to Avraham: Why did Sarah laugh and say: Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?

18:14 Is anything too hard for YHWH? At the appointed time I will return to you, about this time next year, and Sarah shall have a son.

18:15 But Sarah denied it, saying: I did not laugh — for she was afraid. He said: No, but you did laugh.

18:16 Then the men set out from there, and they looked down toward Sedom. And Avraham went with them to set them on their way.

18:17 YHWH said: Shall I hide from Avraham what I am about to do,

18:18 Seeing that Avraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?

18:19 For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of YHWH by doing righteousness and justice, so that YHWH may bring to Avraham what he has promised him.

18:20 Then YHWH said: Because the outcry against Sedom and Amorah is great and their sin is very grave,

18:21 I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me. And if not, I will know.

18:22 So the men turned from there and went toward Sedom, but Avraham still stood before YHWH.

18:23 Then Avraham drew near and said: Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?

18:24 Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city. Will you then sweep away the place and not spare it for the fifty righteous who are in it?

18:25 Far be it from you to do such a thing, to put the righteous to death with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked. Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?

18:26 And YHWH said: If I find at Sedom fifty righteous in the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.

18:27 Avraham answered and said: Behold, I have undertaken to speak to YHWH, I who am but dust and ashes.

18:28 Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking. Will you destroy the whole city for lack of five? And he said: I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.

18:29 Again he spoke to him and said: Suppose forty are found there. He answered: For the sake of forty I will not do it.

18:30 Then he said: Oh let not YHWH be angry, and I will speak. Suppose thirty are found there. He answered: I will not do it if I find thirty there.

18:31 He said: Behold, I have undertaken to speak to YHWH. Suppose twenty are found there. He answered: For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it.

18:32 Then he said: Oh let not YHWH be angry, and I will speak again but this once. Suppose ten are found there. He answered: For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.

18:33 And YHWH went his way, when he had finished speaking to Avraham, and Avraham returned to his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 18. The intercession of Avraham for Sedom is attested in the DSS fragments.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18:19 — I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of YHWH by doing righteousness and justice. YHWH discloses his intention to Avraham because Avraham is chosen to command his household after him. The covenant creates an obligation of transmission — Avraham must teach his children the way of YHWH. The covenant is not merely personal — it is generational and pedagogical.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18:25 — shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just? Avraham challenges YHWH directly on the grounds of justice. YHWH does not rebuke him for it. YHWH engages the argument. This is the ancient Hebrew theology of prayer — the covenant partner has standing to plead, to argue, to intercede. YHWH welcomes this engagement. The negotiation descends from fifty to ten. No fewer than ten righteous could be found in Sedom.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 19 The Destruction of Sedom — Lot's Escape — The Daughters of Lot

19:1 The two angels came to Sedom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gate of Sedom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them and bowed himself with his face to the earth

19:2 And said: My lords, please turn aside to your servant's house and spend the night and wash your feet. Then you may rise up early and go on your way. They said: No — we will spend the night in the town square.

19:3 But he pressed them strongly, so they turned aside to him and entered his house. And he made them a feast and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.

19:4 But before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sedom, both young and old, all the people to the last man, surrounded the house.

19:5 And they called to Lot: Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, that we may know them.

19:6 Lot went out to the men at the entrance, shut the door after him,

19:7 And said: I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly.

19:9 But they said: Stand back. This fellow came to sojourn, and he has become the judge. Now we will deal worse with you than with them. Then they pressed hard against the man Lot, and drew near to break the door down.

19:10 But the men reached out their hands and brought Lot into the house with them and shut the door.

19:11 And they struck with blindness the men who were at the entrance of the house, both small and great, so that they wore themselves out groping for the door.

19:12 Then the men said to Lot: Have you anyone else here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city — bring them out of the place.

19:13 For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before YHWH, and YHWH has sent us to destroy it.

19:14 So Lot went out and said to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters: Up! Get out of this place, for YHWH is about to destroy the city. But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be jesting.

19:15 As morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying: Up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city.

19:16 But he lingered. So the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, YHWH being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.

19:17 And as they brought them out, one said: Escape for your life. Do not look back or stop anywhere in the valley. Escape to the hills, lest you be swept away.

19:24 Then YHWH rained on Sedom and Amorah sulfur and fire from YHWH out of heaven.

19:25 And he overthrew those cities, and all the valley, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.

19:26 But Lot's wife, behind him, looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.

19:27 And Avraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before YHWH.

19:28 And he looked down toward Sedom and Amorah and toward all the land of the valley, and he looked and behold, the smoke of the land went up like the smoke of a furnace.

19:29 So it was that, when The Creator destroyed the cities of the valley, The Creator remembered Avraham and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow when he overthrew the cities in which Lot had lived.

19:30 Now Lot went up out of Tzoar and lived in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to live in Tzoar. So he lived in a cave with his two daughters.

19:36 Thus both the daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.

19:37 The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moav — he is the father of the Moavim to this day.

19:38 The younger also bore a son and called his name Ben-Ami — he is the father of the Ammonites to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 19. The destruction of Sedom is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:16 — he lingered. So the men seized him by the hand, YHWH being merciful to him. Lot hesitated even when angels came to save him. He was pulled out of Sedom by the hands of angels — YHWH's mercy overriding Lot's own inaction. The survival of Lot's family was not due to their virtue or urgency — it was due entirely to the mercy of The Creator acting on Avraham's prayer from Chapter 18. Avraham's intercession saved Lot even though fewer than ten righteous were found.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 20 Avraham and Avimelekh — Sarah Endangered Again

20:1 From there Avraham journeyed toward the territory of the Negev and lived between Kadesh and Shur, and he sojourned in Gerar.

20:2 And Avraham said of Sarah his wife: She is my sister. And Avimelekh king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.

20:3 But The Creator came to Avimelekh in a dream by night and said to him: Behold, you are a dead man because of the woman whom you have taken, for she is a man's wife.

20:4 Now Avimelekh had not approached her. So he said: YHWH, will you kill an innocent people?

20:5 Did he not himself say to me: She is my sister? And she herself said: He is my brother. In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this.

20:6 Then The Creator said to him in the dream: Yes, I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her.

20:7 Now then, return the man's wife, for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you shall live. But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours.

20:8 So Avimelekh rose early in the morning and called all his servants and told them all these things. And the men were very much afraid.

20:9 Then Avimelekh called Avraham and said to him: What have you done to us? And how have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and my kingdom a great sin? You have done to me things that ought not to be done.

20:10 And Avimelekh said to Avraham: What did you see, that you did this thing?

20:11 Avraham said: I did it because I thought, There is no fear of The Creator at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.

20:12 Besides, she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father though not the daughter of my mother, and she became my wife.

20:14 Then Avimelekh took sheep and oxen, and male servants and female servants, and gave them to Avraham, and returned Sarah his wife to him.

20:17 Then Avraham prayed to The Creator, and The Creator healed Avimelekh, and also healed his wife and female slaves so that they bore children.

20:18 For YHWH had closed all the wombs of the house of Avimelekh because of Sarah, Avraham's wife.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 20.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20:7 — he is a prophet, and he will pray for you. YHWH calls Avraham a prophet — navi — for the first time here. Not a patriarch. Not a covenant holder. A prophet. The prophetic office in the ancient record includes the role of intercessor — one who prays on behalf of others. Avraham's prayer heals Avimelekh's household. The covenant holder prays. YHWH acts.

THE BIRTH OF YITZHAK — CHAPTERS 21 THROUGH 23

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 21 The Birth of Yitzhak — Hagar and Yishma'el Sent Away — The Well of Sheva

21:1 YHWH visited Sarah as he had said, and YHWH did to Sarah as he had promised.

21:2 And Sarah conceived and bore Avraham a son in his old age at the time of which The Creator had spoken to him.

21:3 Avraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore him, Yitzhak.

21:4 And Avraham circumcised his son Yitzhak when he was eight days old, as The Creator had commanded him.

21:5 Avraham was a hundred years old when his son Yitzhak was born to him.

21:6 And Sarah said: The Creator has made laughter for me — everyone who hears will laugh over me.

21:7 And she said: Who would have said to Avraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.

21:8 And the child grew and was weaned. And Avraham made a great feast on the day that Yitzhak was weaned.

21:9 But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Avraham, laughing.

21:10 So she said to Avraham: Cast out this slave woman with her son, for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Yitzhak.

21:11 And this thing was very displeasing to Avraham on account of his son.

21:12 But The Creator said to Avraham: Be not displeased because of the boy and because of your slave woman. Whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for through Yitzhak shall your offspring be named.

21:13 And I will make a nation of the son of the slave woman also, because he is your offspring.

21:14 So Avraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a skin of water and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed and wandered in the wilderness of Beer-Sheva.

21:15 When the water in the skin was gone, she put the child under one of the bushes.

21:16 Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot, for she said: Let me not look on the death of the child. And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.

21:17 And The Creator heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of The Creator called to Hagar from heaven and said to her: What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not, for The Creator has heard the voice of the boy where he is.

21:18 Up! Lift up the boy, and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make him into a great nation.

21:19 Then The Creator opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. And she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.

21:20 And The Creator was with the boy, and he grew up. He lived in the wilderness and became an expert with the bow.

21:21 He lived in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.

21:22 At that time Avimelekh and Phicol the commander of his army said to Avraham: The Creator is with you in all that you do.

21:23 Now therefore swear to me here by The Creator that you will not deal falsely with me or with my descendants or with my posterity, but as I have dealt kindly with you, you will deal kindly with me and with the land where you have sojourned.

21:24 And Avraham said: I will swear.

21:25 When Avraham reproved Avimelekh about a well of water that Avimelekh's servants had seized,

21:26 Avimelekh said: I do not know who has done this thing — you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.

21:27 So Avraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Avimelekh, and the two men made a covenant.

21:28 Avraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.

21:29 And Avimelekh said to Avraham: What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?

21:30 He said: These seven ewe lambs you will take from my hand, that this may be a witness for me that I dug this well.

21:31 Therefore that place was called Beer-Sheva, because there both of them swore an oath.

21:32 So they made a covenant at Beer-Sheva. Then Avimelekh and Phicol the commander of his army rose up and returned to the land of the Philistines.

21:33 Avraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-Sheva and called there on the name of YHWH, the Everlasting Creator.

21:34 And Avraham sojourned many days in the land of the Philistines.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 21. The birth of Yitzhak and the covenant at Beer-Sheva are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21:17 — The Creator heard the voice of the boy where he is. Not where Hagar thought he was. Not where the situation appeared to be. YHWH heard the boy where he actually was. This phrase — where he is — is a statement of YHWH's precise knowledge. Hagar could not see a way forward. YHWH opened her eyes to what was already there — verse 21:19. The well was not created in that moment. It was already there. YHWH opened her eyes to see it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21:33 — Avraham called on the name of YHWH the Everlasting Creator. The name El Olam — The Everlasting Creator — appears here for the first time. It is spoken at Beer-Sheva, the place of the covenant with Avimelekh. The everlasting nature of YHWH is invoked at the moment of covenant-making — a witness who cannot die, whose memory cannot be erased, whose commitment cannot expire.

THE AQEDAH — THE BINDING OF YITZHAK — CHAPTER 22

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 22 The Aqedah — The Binding of Yitzhak — The Covenant Oath

22:1 After these things The Creator tested Avraham and said to him: Avraham. And he said: Here I am.

22:2 He said: Take your son, your only son Yitzhak, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I shall tell you.

22:3 So Avraham rose early in the morning, saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, and his son Yitzhak. And he cut the wood for the burnt offering and arose and went to the place of which The Creator had told him.

22:4 On the third day Avraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place from afar.

22:5 Then Avraham said to his young men: Stay here with the donkey. I and the boy will go over there and worship and come again to you.

22:6 And Avraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on Yitzhak his son. And he took in his hand the fire and the knife. So they went both of them together.

22:7 And Yitzhak said to his father Avraham: My father. And he said: Here I am, my son. He said: Behold, the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?

22:8 Avraham said: The Creator will provide for himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son. So they went both of them together.

22:9 When they came to the place of which The Creator had told him, Avraham built the altar there and laid the wood in order and bound Yitzhak his son and laid him on the altar, on top of the wood.

22:10 Then Avraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.

22:11 But the angel of YHWH called to him from heaven and said: Avraham, Avraham. And he said: Here I am.

22:12 He said: Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear The Creator, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.

22:13 And Avraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram, caught in a thicket by his horns. And Avraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.

22:14 So Avraham called the name of that place: YHWH will provide — as it is said to this day, On the mount of YHWH it shall be provided.

22:15 And the angel of YHWH called to Avraham a second time from heaven

22:16 And said: By myself I have sworn, declares YHWH, because you have done this and have not withheld your son, your only son,

22:17 I will surely bless you, and I will surely multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and as the sand that is on the seashore. And your offspring shall possess the gate of his enemies,

22:18 And in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because you have obeyed my voice.

22:19 So Avraham returned to his young men, and they arose and went together to Beer-Sheva. And Avraham lived at Beer-Sheva.

22:20 Now after these things it was told to Avraham: Behold, Milkah also has borne children to your brother Nahor:

22:21 Utz his firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,

22:22 Kesed, Hazo, Pildash, Yidlaf, and Betuel.

22:23 Betuel fathered Rivkah. These eight Milkah bore to Nahor, Avraham's brother.

22:24 Moreover, his concubine, whose name was Re'umah, bore Tevah, Gaham, Tahash, and Ma'akah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 22. The Aqedah — the binding of Yitzhak — is one of the most carefully preserved accounts in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The angel of YHWH calling from heaven twice is attested in the DSS text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Yovelim 17:15-18:19 expands the Aqedah extensively — Mastema, the adversarial angel, is the one who brought the test before YHWH as a challenge against Avraham, paralleling the account of Iyov. YHWH permitted the test to demonstrate Avraham's faithfulness. Yovelim also establishes that the day of the Aqedah became the feast of Pesach — the first of Nisan — the day YHWH provided.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22:2 — take your son, your only son Yitzhak, whom you love. The repetition — your son, your only son, whom you love — is a rhetorical intensification found in the DSS and Orit Ge'ez identically. YHWH names exactly what Avraham values most before asking for it. The ancient record does not soften this. The test is as severe as it appears.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22:8 — The Creator will provide for himself the lamb. Avraham speaks prophetically without knowing it. He did not say YHWH has already provided. He said YHWH will provide. This verb form in the Hebrew is future — yet it is also read in the ancient tradition as present — YHWH sees to it himself. The mountain of the Aqedah is identified in the ancient record as Moriah — the same location as the Temple Mount in Yerushalayim. Every Temple sacrifice made on that mountain was a continuation of what Avraham declared: YHWH will provide for himself the offering on this mountain.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22:16 — by myself I have sworn. YHWH swears by himself because there is nothing greater by which to swear. This is the covenant oath that seals everything. It is unconditional. It is irrevocable. The blessing of all nations through Avraham's offspring — confirmed here by a divine oath — is the theological foundation upon which the entire prophetic literature of the ancient Hebrew record stands.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 23 The Death of Sarah — The Cave of Makhpelah

23:1 Sarah lived 127 years — these were the years of the life of Sarah.

23:2 And Sarah died at Kiryat-Arba, that is, Hebron, in the land of Kena'an, and Avraham went in to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.

23:3 And Avraham rose up from before his dead and said to the Hittites:

23:4 I am a sojourner and foreigner among you — give me property among you for a burying place, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.

23:5 The Hittites answered Avraham:

23:6 Hear us, my lord — you are a prince of The Creator among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will withhold from you his tomb to hinder you from burying your dead.

23:7 Avraham rose and bowed to the Hittites, the people of the land.

23:8 And he said to them: If you are willing that I should bury my dead out of my sight, hear me and entreat for me Efron the son of Tzohar,

23:9 That he may give me the cave of Makhpelah, which he owns — it is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence as property for a burying place.

23:10 Now Efron was sitting among the Hittites, and Efron the Hittite answered Avraham in the hearing of the Hittites, of all who went in at the gate of his city:

23:11 No, my lord, hear me — I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it. In the sight of the sons of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead.

23:12 Then Avraham bowed down before the people of the land.

23:13 And he said to Efron in the hearing of the people of the land: But if you will, hear me — I give the price of the field. Accept it from me, that I may bury my dead there.

23:14 Efron answered Avraham:

23:15 My lord, listen to me — a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver, what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.

23:16 Avraham listened to Efron, and Avraham weighed out for Efron the silver that he had named in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver, according to the weights current among the merchants.

23:17 So the field of Efron in Makhpelah, which was to the east of Mamre, the field with the cave that was in it and all the trees that were in the field, throughout its whole area, was made over

23:18 To Avraham as a possession in the presence of the Hittites, before all who went in at the gate of his city.

23:19 After this, Avraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Makhpelah east of Mamre, that is, Hebron, in the land of Kena'an.

23:20 The field and the cave that is in it were made over to Avraham as property for a burying place by the Hittites.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 23.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Yovelim 19:1-9 records that Avraham mourned for Sarah for seven days before negotiating for the burial cave — the origin of the seven-day mourning period in ancient Hebrew practice.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23:4 — I am a sojourner and foreigner among you. Avraham — who has received the covenant promise of the whole land — nonetheless identifies himself as a sojourner when it comes to acquiring land through purchase. He owns nothing in Kena'an by legal deed despite the covenant promise. He pays full market price for one cave. The covenant promise of the land is held in faith. The first legally purchased ground in the promised land is a burial cave. This is the ancient record's statement about covenant faith — it claims the whole while holding almost nothing.

THE WIFE FOR YITZHAK — CHAPTER 24

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 24 Avraham Sends for a Wife for Yitzhak — Rivkah

24:1 Now Avraham was old, well advanced in years. And YHWH had blessed Avraham in all things.

24:2 And Avraham said to his servant, the oldest of his household, who had charge of all that he had: Put your hand under my thigh,

24:3 That I may make you swear by YHWH, The Creator of heaven and The Creator of earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Kena'ani, among whom I dwell,

24:4 But will go to my country and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son Yitzhak.

24:5 The servant said to him: Perhaps the woman may not be willing to follow me to this land. Must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?

24:6 Avraham said to him: See to it that you do not take my son back there.

24:7 YHWH, The Creator of heaven, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my kindred, and who spoke to me and swore to me: To your offspring I will give this land — he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.

24:8 But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be free from this oath of mine — only you must not take my son back there.

24:9 So the servant put his hand under the thigh of Avraham his master and swore to him concerning this matter.

24:10 Then the servant took ten of his master's camels and departed, taking all sorts of choice gifts from his master. And he arose and went to Aram-Naharaim, to the city of Nahor.

24:11 And he made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water at the time of evening, the time when women go out to draw water.

24:12 And he said: O YHWH, The Creator of my master Avraham, please grant me success today and show steadfast love to my master Avraham.

24:13 Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.

24:14 Let the young woman to whom I shall say: Please let down your jar that I may drink, and who shall say: Drink, and I will water your camels — let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Yitzhak. By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master.

24:15 Before he had finished speaking, behold, Rivkah, who was born to Betuel the son of Milkah, the wife of Nahor, Avraham's brother, came out with her water jar on her shoulder.

24:16 The young woman was very attractive in appearance, a maiden whom no man had known. She went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up.

24:17 Then the servant ran to meet her and said: Please give me a little water to drink from your jar.

24:18 She said: Drink, my lord. And she quickly let down her jar upon her hand and gave him a drink.

24:19 When she had finished giving him a drink, she said: I will draw water for your camels also, until they have finished drinking.

24:20 So she quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw water, and she drew for all his camels.

24:21 The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether YHWH had prospered his journey or not.

24:22 When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold ring weighing a half shekel, and two bracelets for her arms weighing ten gold shekels,

24:23 And said: Please tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your father's house for us to spend the night?

24:24 She said to him: I am the daughter of Betuel the son of Milkah, whom she bore to Nahor.

24:25 She added: We have plenty of both straw and fodder, and room to spend the night.

24:26 The man bowed his head and worshiped YHWH

24:27 And said: Blessed be YHWH, The Creator of my master Avraham, who has not forsaken his steadfast love and his faithfulness toward my master. As for me, YHWH has led me in the way to the house of my master's kinsmen.

24:28 Then the young woman ran and told her mother's household about these things.

24:29 Rivkah had a brother whose name was Lavan. Lavan ran out toward the man, to the spring.

24:33 And food was set before him to eat. But he said: I will not eat until I have said what I have to say. He said: Speak on.

24:34 So he said: I am Avraham's servant.

24:35 YHWH has greatly blessed my master, and he has become great. He has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male servants and female servants, camels and donkeys.

24:36 And Sarah my master's wife bore a son to my master when she was old, and to him he has given all that he has.

24:42 I came today to the spring and said: O YHWH, The Creator of my master Avraham, if now you are prospering the way that I go,

24:50 Then Lavan and Betuel answered and said: The thing has come from YHWH — we cannot speak to you bad or good.

24:51 Behold, Rivkah is before you — take her and go, and let her be the wife of your master's son, as YHWH has spoken.

24:55 Her brother and her mother said: Let the young woman remain with us a while, at least ten days — after that she may go.

24:56 But he said to them: Do not delay me, since YHWH has prospered my way. Send me away that I may go to my master.

24:57 They said: Let us call the young woman and ask her.

24:58 And they called Rivkah and said to her: Will you go with this man? She said: I will go.

24:60 And they blessed Rivkah and said to her: Our sister, may you become thousands of ten thousands, and may your offspring possess the gate of those who hate him.

24:61 Then Rivkah and her young women arose and rode on the camels and followed the man. Thus the servant took Rivkah and went his way.

24:62 Now Yitzhak had returned from Beer-lahai-roi and was dwelling in the Negev.

24:63 And Yitzhak went out to meditate in the field toward evening. And he lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, there were camels coming.

24:64 And Rivkah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Yitzhak, she dismounted from the camel

24:65 And said to the servant: Who is that man, walking in the field to meet us? The servant said: It is my master. So she took her veil and covered herself.

24:66 And the servant told Yitzhak all the things that he had done.

24:67 Then Yitzhak brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother and took Rivkah, and she became his wife, and he loved her. So Yitzhak was comforted after his mother's death.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 24. The account of the servant finding Rivkah is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24:14 — the servant's prayer is a model of covenant intercession in the ancient record. He does not tell YHWH what outcome he wants. He asks YHWH to confirm through a sign what YHWH has already appointed. The verb appointed — haqhita — implies YHWH had already chosen before the servant prayed. The prayer was not requesting a new decision from YHWH but asking to recognize the one already made.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24:67 — Yitzhak brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother and he loved her. The covenant line continues not merely through biological descent but through love and chosen commitment. Yitzhak was comforted after his mother's death by Rivkah. The Torah names this love explicitly. Yovelim 19:10 records that Yitzhak rejoiced and blessed YHWH for Rivkah and that she was worthy in his eyes and the eyes of his father.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 25 The Death of Avraham — The Sons of Yishma'el — The Birth of Esav and Yaakov

25:1 Avraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.

25:2 She bore him Zimran, Yokshan, Medan, Midyan, Yishbak, and Shuach.

25:3 Yokshan fathered Sheva and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Ashurim, Letushim, and Leummim.

25:4 The sons of Midyan were Efah, Efer, Hanokh, Avida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.

25:5 Avraham gave all he had to Yitzhak.

25:6 But to the sons of his concubines Avraham gave gifts, and while he was still living he sent them away from his son Yitzhak, eastward to the east country.

25:7 These are the days of the years of Avraham's life, 175 years.

25:8 Avraham breathed his last and died in a good old age, an old man and full of years, and was gathered to his people.

25:9 Yitzhak and Yishma'el his sons buried him in the cave of Makhpelah, in the field of Efron the son of Tzohar the Hittite, east of Mamre,

25:10 The field that Avraham purchased from the Hittites. There Avraham was buried, with Sarah his wife.

25:11 After the death of Avraham, The Creator blessed Yitzhak his son. And Yitzhak settled at Beer-lahai-roi.

25:12 These are the generations of Yishma'el, Avraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's servant, bore to Avraham.

25:13 These are the names of the sons of Yishma'el, named in the order of their birth: Nevayot the firstborn of Yishma'el, and Kedar, Adbeel, Mivsam,

25:14 Mishma, Dumah, Massa,

25:15 Hadad, Tema, Yetur, Nafish, and Kedemah.

25:16 These are the sons of Yishma'el and these are their names, by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their tribes.

25:17 These are the years of the life of Yishma'el: 137 years. He breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people.

25:18 They settled from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite Egypt in the direction of Asshur. He settled over against all his kinsmen.

25:19 These are the generations of Yitzhak, Avraham's son. Avraham fathered Yitzhak,

25:20 And Yitzhak was forty years old when he took Rivkah, the daughter of Betuel the Aramean of Paddan-Aram, the sister of Lavan the Aramean, to be his wife.

25:21 And Yitzhak prayed to YHWH for his wife, because she was barren. And YHWH granted his prayer, and Rivkah his wife conceived.

25:22 The children struggled together within her, and she said: If it is thus, why is this happening to me? So she went to inquire of YHWH.

25:23 And YHWH said to her: Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from within you shall be divided — the one shall be stronger than the other, the older shall serve the younger.

25:24 When her days to give birth were completed, behold, there were twins in her womb.

25:25 The first came out red, all his body like a hairy cloak, so they called his name Esav.

25:26 Afterward his brother came out with his hand holding Esav's heel, so his name was called Yaakov. Yitzhak was sixty years old when she bore them.

25:27 When the boys grew up, Esav was a skillful hunter, a man of the field, while Yaakov was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.

25:28 Yitzhak loved Esav because he ate of his game, but Rivkah loved Yaakov.

25:29 Once when Yaakov was cooking stew, Esav came in from the field, and he was exhausted.

25:30 And Esav said to Yaakov: Let me eat some of that red stew, for I am exhausted. Therefore his name was called Edom.

25:31 Yaakov said: Sell me your birthright now.

25:32 Esav said: I am about to die — of what use is a birthright to me?

25:33 Yaakov said: Swear to me now. So he swore to him and sold his birthright to Yaakov.

25:34 Then Yaakov gave Esav bread and lentil stew, and he ate and drank and rose and went his way. Thus Esav despised his birthright.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 25 including the birth of Esav and Yaakov. The Cave 4 manuscripts attest this passage.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full. Yovelim 19:13-15 records Avraham's final blessing of Yitzhak before his death and states that Avraham blessed The Creator who had created him and sustained him all the days of his life. Yovelim 19:17-25 records Avraham's specific last words and blessings to Yaakov directly — singling out Yaakov as the continuation of the covenant line.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:8 — Avraham breathed his last and was gathered to his people. The phrase gathered to his people is used throughout the Torah for the patriarchs and Moshe. It implies a reunion — not simply death and burial. The ancient record understands that Avraham went to join those who had gone before him. This phrase connects to the four compartments of the realm of the dead described in Chanokh Chapter 22 — the righteous are gathered to a specific place, the place with the bright spring of water.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:23 — the older shall serve the younger. YHWH declares the reversal of birth order before the children are born. The covenant line does not pass through the firstborn automatically. YHWH chooses. Yitzhak was chosen over Yishma'el. Yaakov is chosen over Esav. The covenant bypasses the expected natural order throughout Bereshit — this is the ancient record's sustained theological statement: YHWH's covenant operates by divine selection, not human primogeniture.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:34 — Esav despised his birthright. The Torah's judgment on Esav is stated plainly and finally. He was not deceived in this moment — no one manipulated him. He was hungry, he made a choice, he despised what was sacred. The ancient record holds him responsible for this. Yovelim 24:7 confirms that Esav willingly sold his birthright and covenant standing for a meal.

END OF BERESHIT PART TWO — VOLUME ONE

Chapters 12 through 25 — The Complete Life of Avraham

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN BERESHIT PART THREE

Chapters 26 through 36 — Yitzhak and Yaakov
